# A failed stage always notifies mess's well-known human mailbox ("user"),
# regardless of pipeline role structure — there's no "next stage" to derive a
# more specific target from, and this is exactly the case that used to depend on a
# separately-run `breeze operator notify` watcher process actually being alive.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state
env PATH=$WORK/bin:$PATH
chmod 0755 bin/mess

exec breeze identity register ci
cp stdout ci.token
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as ci --token-file ci.token

exec breeze stage start release build abc123 --as ci
stdout 'build: failed'

exec sleep 0.5
exists mess-calls.log
grep '^send user breeze: release/build \(abc123\) -> failed$' mess-calls.log

exec breeze stop

-- bin/mess --
#!/bin/sh
echo "$@" >> mess-calls.log

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/false"]
  }
}
